subcodes.first_line: O ye two believers in God! The Lord, peerless is He, hath made woman and man to abide with each other in the closest companionship
subcodes.first_line_orig: ای دو مومن بالله خداوند بی مانند زن و مرد را آفریده که با یکدیگر در نهایت
subcodes.subjects: @marriage as means of spiritual progress, @chastisement and requital
full_text_parts: O ye two believers in God! The Lord, peerless is He, hath made woman and man to abide with each other in the closest companionship, and to be even as a single soul. They are two helpmates, two intimate friends, who should be concerned about the welfare of each other. If they live thus, they will pass through this world with perfect contentment, bliss, and peace of heart, and become the object of divine grace and favor in the Kingdom of heaven. But if they do other than this, they will live out their lives in great bitterness, longing at every moment for death, and will be shamefaced in the heavenly realm. Strive, then, to abide, heart and soul, with each other as two doves in the nest, for this is to be blessed in both worlds.
